codeReview.summary: This fix commit resolves eight of the nine round-1 blockers: canonical castling-right handling, side-to-move-relative evaluation, mate-in-1 motif coverage, the LMR depth gate, aspiration-window retries, WASM opening-book embedding, self-contained `pnpm test`, and worker runtime compatibility are all addressed in the diff. The remaining blocker is repetition handling: `set_position()` now preserves history only for identical positions or one-ply successors, which is still insufficient for the documented frontend flow that calls `set_position(fen)` before `search_best_move()` and then sends the next engine-turn FEN two plies later.

issues.description: `set_position()` only carries history forward when the incoming FEN matches the current position or is a single legal successor (`engine_position_is_successor()` at lines 72-89), but `search_best_move()` (line 153 onward) never advances `engine_position` to the move it just returned. In the documented frontend flow (`.factory/library/architecture.md:95-96,105`), the next `set_position(fen)` arrives after the engine move and the user's reply, i.e. two plies later, so history is reset and threefold repetition is still missed. I confirmed this with a local API harness that mirrored that flow: after repeating the same black-to-move position three times, `evaluate_position()` still returned `-971/-897` instead of `0`.

The new regression coverage only proves repetition preservation when `set_position()` is called on every halfmove (`tests/check-wasm-artifacts.test.mjs:206-223`; native analogue `engine/tests/test_engine_api.c:394-429`). That does not match the documented hook/search flow, where `useChessEngine.searchBestMove()` and `evaluatePosition()` call `setPosition(fen)` only when the frontend explicitly asks for a search/eval (`src/hooks/useChessEngine.ts:97,136`). This coverage gap is why the unresolved frontend repetition bug above still passes the added tests.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared `integration-worker` skill still documents only the ES-module / `import()` worker path, even though this fix intentionally used the allowed classic-worker `importScripts()` fallback and reported that deviation in `skillFeedback`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff `/Users/aravhawk/.factory/missions/70ca98e6-cb3b-4e1c-9eb9-4d887ed17139/handoffs/2026-03-31T06-39-02-311Z__engine-scrutiny-fixes__535beae7-b932-443a-a08f-33934cd5cd8d.json` records the classic-worker deviation and suggested updating the skill; `.factory/skills/integration-worker/SKILL.md:66-71` still says the worker uses `import('/engine/engine.js')` and therefore requires `-s EXPORT_ES6=1`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared architecture note is now out of sync with the runtime worker bootstrap: it still says the worker loads `engine.js` via dynamic import, but the actual implementation now uses `importScripts()`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/architecture.md:44-45` says the Web Worker loads the WASM module via dynamic import of `engine.js`, while `src/workers/chess-engine.worker.ts:53-63` uses `self.importScripts("/engine/engine.js")` and reads `createChessEngine` from the worker global scope.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared engine API note still claims the WASM build only uses the tiny embedded fallback book, but this fix now embeds and loads the real `rodent.bin` book inside the WASM filesystem.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/engine-book-api.md:3` says the `__EMSCRIPTEN__` path uses the embedded fallback directly; `engine/src/book.c:231-237` now tries `book_load_file("/book.bin")` first, and `scripts/build-engine.sh:31-32` embeds `engine/book/rodent.bin@/book.bin`.

summary: Reviewed the fix handoff, transcript skeleton, commit `88b94482297e1e6ae1117ce409f611505d8e206c`, and the prior failed reviews/synthesis. Result: fail. The commit resolves the FEN normalization, eval sign, mate-coverage, LMR, aspiration, WASM book, pretest, and worker-loading blockers, but the repetition-history fix is still incomplete for the documented `set_position(fen)` → `search_best_move()` frontend flow because history is only preserved across identical or one-ply-successor FENs.
